title: Kerberoasting Workflow (GetUserSPNs / Rubeus)
description: This runbook operationalizes Kerberoasting Workflow (GetUserSPNs / Rubeus) using #getuserspns.py , #rubeus , #hashcat . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Requests service tickets for SPN accounts and cracks TGS hashes offline.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Kerberoasting Workflow (GetUserSPNs / Rubeus) using #getuserspns.py , #rubeus , #hashcat. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Requests service tickets for SPN accounts and cracks TGS hashes offline.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Active Directory Enumeration and Attacks.md:164; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:434.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP/UDP 88.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

GetUserSPNs.py -dc-ip <DC_IP> <DOMAIN>/<USER> -request-user <SPN_USER> -outputfile tgs_hashes
.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /outfile:hashes.kerberoast
hashcat -m 13100 tgs_hashes /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt
extraInfo: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md ; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/0x1ceKing-HTB-Certified-Penetration-Testing-Specialist/cheatsheet/Active Directory Enumeration and Attacks.md:164; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/hackmd-oscp-pen200.md:434 .
prerequisites: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP/UDP 88 .

steps.name: Preparation and Target Validation
steps.description: Operator initializes the workflow and verifies prerequisites for Kerberoasting Workflow (GetUserSPNs / Rubeus).
steps.tooltip: Early validation reduces noise and prevents invalid follow-on actions against unreachable or mismatched targets.

steps.name: Primary Execution
steps.description: Main command path executes against the target service or domain component.
steps.tooltip: The technique leverages service behavior, protocol assumptions, or misconfiguration to extract actionable state.

steps.name: Expansion and Verification
steps.description: Follow-up commands validate initial findings and expand result coverage.
steps.tooltip: Secondary checks reduce false positives and expose deeper privilege or access pathways.

steps.name: Consolidation for Follow-On Operations
steps.description: Final command stage consolidates output for cracking, pivoting, reporting, or escalation decisions.
steps.tooltip: Structured artifact capture enables deterministic downstream runbooks and profile ingestion.
